title: sorbus maderensis
intro: Sorbus maderensis, also known as the Madeira whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the western Atlantic islands, including the Madeira Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with its bark and leaves containing compounds such as sorbic acid. The plant's medicinal properties have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiviral, and antifungal effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Sorbus maderensis is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
